sound_type: pump-er-LUNK booming -- deep resonant mechanical boom created by gulping air into esophagus; 100-400 Hz; carries 400-800m; E=4
ecosystem: Dense freshwater marsh -- cattails, bulrushes, sedges, wet meadows. Requires large (10+ hectare), undisturbed marshes.
season: Summer breeder in western Washington. Peak booming spring (March-June). Crepuscular.
family: Ardeidae (1st SPS entry -- FIRST ARDEIDAE IN SPS)

coordinates.description: American Bittern in large freshwater marshes of Puget Sound lowlands. Dense cattails, bulrushes. Uncommon and local.

taxonomy_note.description: FIRST ARDEIDAE IN SPS. The heron family enters. American Bittern is the cryptic, invisible heron -- stake posture, dense marsh, pump-er-LUNK booming. Contrast with conspicuous Great Blue Heron.
taxonomy_note.name_etymology: Botaurus from Latin 'bos' (ox) + 'taurus' (bull) -- bull-like booming. lentiginosus from Latin 'lentiginosus' (freckled) -- referring to plumage markings.
taxonomy_note.genus_note: Botaurus -- the bitterns. Large, cryptic, marsh-dwelling herons. Worldwide.
taxonomy_note.order_note: Pelecaniformes. First entry of this order in the engine.
taxonomy_note.family_position: First Ardeidae in SPS. The invisible heron. Pump-er-LUNK booming.

song_metrics.fundamental_frequency_range_hz: 100-400 Hz. Some energy below 100 Hz. Lowest in marsh soundscape.
song_metrics.peak_energy_band_hz: 150-300 Hz. The 'LUNK' element carries the most energy.
song_metrics.song_duration_s: Each phrase: 1-2 seconds. 3-7 repetitions per bout. Sessions: 1-5 minutes.
song_metrics.note_structure: PUMP-ER-LUNK or OONG-KA-CHUNK: Three syllables. Quiet intake (pump), connective (er), explosive boom (LUNK). The boom is the loudest and most resonant element.
song_metrics.note_types: Pump-er-LUNK booming (primary -- territorial), flight call 'kok-kok-kok' (rarely heard), quiet hissing (alarm, close range).
song_metrics.phrase_structure: Three-syllable phrase repeated 3-7 times. Produced from concealed position in dense marsh.
song_metrics.timbre: Deep, resonant, mechanical. Sounds like a water pump, stake driven into mud, or distant thunder. Utterly unlike any other bird sound.
song_metrics.amplitude: E=4. Carries 400-800 meters across open marsh. Up to 1 mile under calm conditions. Extraordinary for a wading bird.
song_metrics.tempo: Slow. Each phrase deliberate. Pauses between repetitions.
song_metrics.context: Territorial announcement. Breeding display. Dawn/dusk. Some nocturnal. March-June peak.
song_metrics.key_property: PUMP-ER-LUNK -- esophageal mechanical booming. FIRST ARDEIDAE IN SPS. 100-400 Hz (lowest in marsh). 400-800m carry. Stake camouflage posture. E=4. v1.49.303.

plumage.overall: Warm brown with heavy vertical streaking on breast and throat. Cryptic in marsh -- streaks align with reeds during stake posture.
plumage.diagnostic_mark: Vertical breast streaking (aligns with reeds). Large (65-85 cm). Stocky heron build. Yellow-green bill. Green legs.
plumage.seasonal_variation: Minimal. White pectoral plumes visible during booming display.
plumage.flight_pattern: Strong flight with broad, rounded wings. Brown above, shows dark flight feathers. Rarely seen in flight.

physical_description.overall: Length: 58-85 cm. Weight: 370-500 g. Stocky, heron-like build. Large but cryptic.
physical_description.diagnostic_mark: Vertical streaking. Stake posture. Stocky build. Dense marsh habitat.
physical_description.field_id: Usually detected by pump-er-LUNK booming from marsh. If seen: large stocky brown bird with vertical streaking, standing motionless in reeds, often in stake posture with bill pointed skyward.

nodes.title: Pump-er-LUNK Booming
nodes.description: Deep resonant mechanical boom. Esophageal air gulping. 100-400 Hz. Carries 400-800m. Three syllables: intake, connective, explosive boom. One of the strangest sounds in nature.

nodes.title: Esophageal Sound Production
nodes.description: NOT syringeal. Male gulps air into expanded esophagus as resonance chamber. Neck visibly swells. Unique in N.A. birds. Mechanical sound production.

nodes.title: Stake Camouflage Posture
nodes.description: Bill pointed skyward. Neck extended. Rigid. Breast streaks align with reeds. Sways with wind. Eyes see forward. Holds for minutes. Effective within arm's reach.

nodes.title: Large Undisturbed Marsh Requirement
nodes.description: Requires 10+ hectare contiguous marsh. Dense cattails/bulrushes. Sensitive to disturbance. Declining with wetland loss. Conservation concern in Washington.

nodes.title: First Ardeidae in SPS
nodes.description: The heron family enters. The invisible heron -- contrast with conspicuous Great Blue. Pump-er-LUNK booming as marsh voice. Stake posture as camouflage.

cross_references.relationship: MARSH COHABITANTS! Both in same freshwater marsh. Both secretive. Different families. Whinny grunt vs. pump-er-LUNK. Skulking vs. stake posture.
MARSH SOUNDSCAPE! Sora keek (3000-6000 Hz) + Bittern boom (100-400 Hz). Complementary frequency bands. Complete marsh acoustic range.
CAMOUFLAGE COMPARISON! Mountain Quail runs into brush. Bittern freezes in stake posture. Two strategies for invisibility.
